title: 10 best herbal tinctures for itchy eyes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce redness and swelling associated with irritated eyes. The herb's active compounds, such as echinacea and chlorogenic acid, work to calm and soothe the delicate tissues around the eyes, providing long-lasting relief from itchiness and discomfort. As a natural remedy, purple coneflower tincture is an effective alternative to conventional eye care products, offering a gentle and non-irritating solution for itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes caused by allergic reactions or eye strain.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in purple coneflower tincture help neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and cause itchiness, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall eye health.

Anti-allergic property: Purple coneflower's anti-allergic compounds have been shown to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for allergic reactions, which can alleviate itchy eyes caused by environmental allergens.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help soothe itchy eyes and reduce redness.
Seeds: The seeds contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in alleviating itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers using a clean towel to remove excess moisture.
Combine 100 grams of dried echinacea flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks to allow for optimal extraction time.
Strain and filter the tincture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids before transferring to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with itchy eyes because they contain a potent anti-inflammatory compound called flavonoid, which soothes and calms irritated eye tissues. This natural remedy also has antimicrobial properties that help reduce redness and inflammation caused by bacterial or viral infections. When taken as a tincture, the active compounds in pot marigold quickly absorb into the bloodstream, providing fast relief from itchy eyes and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tincture contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that can reduce inflammation in the eyes, which helps to alleviate itchiness and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tincture help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eyes, which may contribute to itchy eyes.

Soothing and cooling effect: Pot marigold tincture has a soothing and cooling effect on the eyes, which can provide quick relief from itchiness and discomfort caused by dryness, allergies, or other irritants.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Flowers: The flowers of the pot marigold plant have anti-irritant properties that help reduce redness and itching associated with irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers, selecting only the bright yellow and orange varieties for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers by gently shaking off excess moisture and spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels.
Combine one part dried or 2 parts fresh calendula flowers with two-thirds part vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the herb is completely submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the mixture daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and using the resulting liquid at a dosage of 20-30 drops per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which calm irritated ocular tissues. The herb's natural astringent qualities help reduce redness and swelling, while its antibacterial properties combat infections that can cause itchiness. Additionally, eyebright's moisturizing effects help to hydrate the eyes, relieving dryness and discomfort that often accompanies itchy eyes. As a result, eyebright tinctures provide fast and effective relief from itchy eyes, making them a popular natural remedy for soothing irritated peepers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Eyebright's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Antihistamine properties: Eyebright contains histamine-blocking compounds that can help alleviate allergic reactions, reducing itching, redness, and discharge caused by allergies or irritants.

Astringent properties: Eyebright's astringent compounds help reduce excess mucus production and congestion in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort associated with dry eye syndrome or conjunctivitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of bitter compounds, which help to reduce inflammation and ease itchiness in the eyes.
Leaves: The leaves contain a significant amount of iridoid glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers for optimal potency.
Chop the fresh herb material into small pieces and fill a clean glass jar to the brim.
Pour 100-proof vodka over the chopped herb material, covering them completely by about an inch.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking it daily for 15 seconds each time.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Watercress tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herbal extract contains flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation in the eye tissues, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, watercress tinctures have natural antihistamine properties that combat histamine release, a common cause of itchy eyes. By applying the tincture to the affected area, users can experience immediate relief from itching and irritation, promoting a sense of comfort and clarity in their vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Watercress contains a variety of anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and antioxidants, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness in the eyes.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant-rich composition of watercress tinctures helps neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye irritation, dryness, and itchiness, promoting a healthier eye environment.
Vasodilatory effects: Watercress contains compounds that help dilate blood vessels, which can improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the eyes, relieving itchy, irritated sensations.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing and calming irritated eye tissues.
Barks: Contains tannins that help reduce inflammation and itchiness by constricting blood vessels and reducing histamine release.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh watercress leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated and before the plants flower.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned watercress into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and transfer to amber glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with itchy eyes because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated eyes. The natural astringent and antioxidant compounds in aloe vera help to reduce redness, swelling, and itchiness by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation. Additionally, the gentle, non-irritating formula of herbal aloe tinctures makes it an effective and safe solution for dry, itchy, or irritated eyes, providing long-lasting relief from discomfort and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation caused by allergic reactions or irritants, providing relief from itchy eyes.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant compounds in aloe tincture neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the eye tissues, reducing inflammation and itchiness.

Soften and calm: Aloe's soothing and moisturizing properties help soften and calm the eyelids and conjunctiva, reducing itchiness and discomfort associated with dryness, irritation, or allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel inside the leaves contains anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Roots: The roots contain antioxidants and flavonoids that work together to reduce inflammation and itchiness in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest aloe vera leaves and wash them thoroughly with distilled water to remove any impurities.
Chop the washed aloe vera leaves into small pieces and soak them in vodka at a ratio of one part leaf to two parts liquid for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the solids from the liquid tincture.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dry place for up to 5 years.
Use 20-30 drops of the tincture in distilled water three times daily as an eye wash to alleviate itchiness.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, work to calm irritated tissues and reduce swelling in the eyes. Additionally, English lavender's gentle, non-irritating nature makes it an ideal solution for sensitive eyes. As a result, users can experience rapid relief from itchy, burning, or watery eyes, promoting overall eye comfort and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine property: Lavender's antihistamine property helps to reduce itching and inflammation caused by allergic reactions or histamine release in the eyes.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of lavender can help to soothe and calm itchy, irritated eyes by reducing swelling and redness.
Anxiolytic (stress-reducing) property: Lavender's anxiolytic effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for itchy eyes, and promote relaxation and calmness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain a high concentration of essential oils and flavonoids, which help to soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Flowers: Lavender flowers are rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that has anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and itching associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh english lavender flowers, carefully collecting at least 2 ounces of fragrant blooms.
Cleanse the harvested flowers by gently rinsing them in cold running water and drying them completely.
Prepare the flowers for tincture making by placing them in a clean glass jar with 80 proof vodka, filling to 90% capacity.
Steep the mixture for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily and monitoring for any signs of mold or spoilage.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and storing the tincture in dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The herb's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and citral, work to reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes, providing quick relief from annoying symptoms. Additionally, lemon balm's soothing and calming effects help to relax the eyelids and conjunctiva, further alleviating discomfort and promoting healthy tear production. This natural remedy can be especially helpful for individuals who experience seasonal allergies or dry eye syndrome.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine-like effects: Lemon balm tincture's antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce itching, redness, and swelling associated with allergies or irritants in the eyes.
Anxiolytic (stress-relieving) properties: The calming effects of lemon balm can help alleviate eye strain caused by prolonged screen time or stress, which may contribute to itchy eyes.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's flavonoids and phenolic acids have been shown to reduce inflammation in the body, including in the eyes, which can help soothe itchy, irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant are used to make medicinal tinctures for itchy eyes because they contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe irritated eyes.

Seeds: Lemon balm seeds are used in medicinal tinctures for itchy eyes due to their unique ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness, making them an effective natural remedy for relieving eye irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves in the early morning or late afternoon to ensure optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cool running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one part dried melissa officinalis leaves with two parts high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids, using the resulting tincture within six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eye tissue. The tincture's botanical extracts gently reduce redness and swelling, providing fast relief from itchiness caused by allergies, dryness, or other environmental factors. Additionally, maidenhair tree contains antioxidants that protect the eyes from further irritation, promoting healthy vision and comfort. By using this natural remedy, individuals can enjoy clear, comfortable vision without the annoyance of itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with itchy eyes.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba tinctures, including vitamin C and beta-carotene, protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchy eye symptoms.

Eye-soothing property: The mucilage content of Ginkgo biloba, a type of natural gum, has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated eyes, providing relief from itchiness, dryness, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing itching and discomfort.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree have been traditionally used to treat eye infections and inflammation, making them a natural remedy for itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees in late autumn or early winter for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat for 24 hours.
Chop 100g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces and soak them in 500ml of 60% ethanol vodka for 14 days.
Strain the tincture mixture through cheesecloth and squeeze out excess liquid before transferring it to dark glass bottles.
Allow the tincture to mature for at least 6 weeks before using it to help alleviate itchy eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Candelilla tinctures helps with itchy eyes because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated eyes. The tincture's ability to reduce swelling and redness provides immediate relief from itchiness, dryness, and discomfort. Additionally, candelilla's natural antihistamine effects help to combat allergic reactions that can cause itchy eyes. Antibacterial and antifungal property: The antibacterial and antifungal compounds in candellila tincture can help combat eye infections and reduce the growth of bacteria or fungi that may be causing itchiness or discomfort.
Anti-allergic property: Candellila's anti-allergic properties can help alleviate itching, redness, and swelling caused by allergic reactions to environmental irritants, such as pollen or dust.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, making them a popular choice for treating itchiness.
Barks: The barks of the candelilla plant contain soothing properties that can reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, providing quick relief from itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphorbia officinarum leaves and flowers, washing them gently with distilled water.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and combine with 60% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to extract the active compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Store the final tincture in a cool dark place, allowing it to mature for at least 2 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The apigenin present in chamomile binds to GABA receptors in the brain, promoting relaxation and reducing stress-related eye tension. Additionally, chamomile's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate any underlying infections or irritants that may be causing itchiness. As a result, herbal chamomile tinctures provide natural relief from itchy eyes, leaving them feeling calm, comfortable, and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchy and inflamed eyes.
Antioxidant: Chamomile's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that may contribute to eye inflammation and irritation, promoting healthy vision.
Calming agent: Chamomile's calming properties can help soothe itchy and irritated eyes by reducing redness and discomfort, promoting relaxation and ease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, helping to relieve itchiness and redness in the eyes.
Flowers: Known for their calming effects on irritated tissues, chamomile flowers help to reduce inflammation and itching in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in quantities of 100 grams.
Combine the dried flowers with 70% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 14 days, shaking the jar daily and allowing it to sit undisturbed at room temperature.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.
Store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for up to one year after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively soothe and calm irritated conjunctiva. The herb's natural compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, have been shown to reduce redness and inflammation in the eyes, providing rapid relief from itchiness and discomfort. Additionally, dandelion tincture's antiseptic properties help eliminate any underlying infection or irritant that may be contributing to the itching sensation, promoting a healthy and comfortable visual experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in dandelion tinctures, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and exacerbate itching, promoting overall eye health.

Immune system modulation: Dandelion's immunomodulatory effects help regulate the immune response in the eyes, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of itchiness, such as redness and discharge.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, the roots help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing itchiness.
Fruits: Containing flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, the fruits have been traditionally used to treat eye inflammation and itching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired dosage.
Combine one part of the dried herb material with two parts of vodka or a neutral grain spirit in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks to allow for maceration and extraction.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the filtered tincture to smaller amber-colored glass bottles and store them in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature of around 40°f for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with itchy eyes because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated eye tissue. The herb contains flavonoids, which are known for their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the body. When used topically as a tincture, St John's Wort helps to alleviate itching and discomfort caused by allergic reactions, dry eye syndrome, or other irritants, leaving eyes feeling cool, calm, and refreshed. Its natural anti-inflammatory properties work wonders in providing quick relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: St John's Wort contains hyperforin, a compound that reduces inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate itchiness and discomfort caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis or dry eye syndrome.

Antioxidant properties: The tannins present in St John's Wort have antioxidant effects, which can help protect the delicate tissues of the eye from oxidative damage, reducing inflammation and itching caused by free radical stress.

Antihistamine properties: St John's Wort contains flavonoids that exhibit antihistaminic activity, helping to reduce histamine release in response to allergens or irritants, which can contribute to itchy eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe itchy eyes.
Leaves: Containing essential oils and terpenes, which provide anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties to alleviate itchiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort leaves and flowers at dawn on a dry day.
Dry the plant material in a warm dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 1 part dried st john's wort with 2 parts high-proof vodka or everclear in a clean glass jar.
Store the tincture mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily, before straining and bottling.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean dropper bottle, using a ratio of 1 part tincture to 2 parts water.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eye tissues. The tannins present in plantain help to reduce swelling and ease discomfort, providing relief from itching and redness. Additionally, plantain's natural antihistamine effects can help to combat allergic reactions that cause itchiness, making it a popular natural remedy for dry, itchy eyes. By reducing inflammation and alleviating itchiness, plantain tinctures provide gentle yet effective relief for itchy eye issues.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eye irritations, soothing the affected area.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of plantain tincture works to combat infections and prevent bacterial or fungal growth that can cause itchy eyes, promoting healing and recovery.

Calming agent: Plantain's calming properties help relax the conjunctival tissues, reducing discomfort, itching, and burning sensations associated with eye irritations, allowing for improved vision and comfort.

Barks: Containing astringent properties, effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves measuring approximately two ounces by weight.
Chop the harvested plantago major leaves into small pieces to facilitate extraction.
Combine the chopped plantago major leaves with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days at room temperature, shaking daily.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and itchiness in the eye area. The natural compounds present in stinging nettle, such as histamine and serotonin, help to soothe and calm irritated eyes. This makes it an effective remedy for conditions like conjunctivitis, dry eye syndrome, and even seasonal allergies that can cause itchy, watery eyes. By reducing inflammation and itching, stinging nettle tinctures provide quick relief from uncomfortable and annoying eye symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as quercetin and beta-sitosterol, reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness.

Antihistamine-like effects: Nettle's flavonoids and phenolic acids have natural antihistamine properties that help to block histamine release, reducing itching and redness associated with allergic reactions or conjunctivitis.

Natural mast cell stabilizer: Stinging nettle contains compounds that stabilize mast cells, which helps to prevent the release of histamines and other chemicals that can cause itching, redness, and watery eyes.

Leaves: Nettle leaves are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to calm and soothe irritated eyes, reducing itchiness and redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves and stems at dawn or just before sunset for optimal potency.
Cleanse the harvested plant material by gently rinsing it with cool filtered water and blotting dry.
Combine 100 grams of the cleaned nettle material with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar every other day to facilitate infusion.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth and discard the solids after filtering, resulting in a 25% tincture strength solution.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling. The tincture's sedative effect also calms the nervous system, alleviating tension and stress that can exacerbate eye irritation. Additionally, valerian has natural antihistamine properties, which help to neutralize histamine release in the eyes, further reducing itching and discomfort. remedy_properties: Antihistamine and anti-inflammatory effects: Valerian's bioactive compounds, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, have been shown to reduce histamine release and inflammation, which can alleviate itching and redness in the eyes.

Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Valerian's ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety can help calm the nervous system, reducing symptoms of dry eye syndrome, irritation, and itchiness that are often exacerbated by stress.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial activity: Valerian's antioxidant compounds may help protect the eyes from oxidative damage and reduce the risk of infections, which can contribute to itchy and irritated eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots have been traditionally used to create soothing and calming tinctures that can help alleviate itchiness and inflammation in the eyes.
Rhizomes: The rhizome, or underground stem, of valerian contains a high concentration of active compounds that can be extracted into a tincture to provide relief from itchy eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots in early summer when they are mature.
Cleanly chop the harvested roots into small pieces and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 10 parts of the ground valeriana root powder with 20 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for six to eight weeks at room temperature, shaking the jar daily during preparation time.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle and discard the solids after pressing the filter to remove any remaining particles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregon grape tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The botanicals in the tincture, such as berberine, have been shown to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing quick relief from itchy and irritated eyes. Berberine: This alkaloid has antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which can help combat underlying infections or irritants that may be causing itchy eyes, such as conjunctivitis or allergies.
Flavonoids: These bioactive compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm irritated eye tissues, reducing itchiness and discomfort.

Bark: The bark of the Oregon Grape plant has been traditionally used to treat various skin conditions, including itchiness, due to its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh berberis aquifolium leaves and stems in the morning or late afternoon.
Chop the plant material into small pieces and combine with 50-60% vodka by weight.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and allow it to steep for 4-6 weeks.
Shake the jar daily for 30 seconds to ensure proper infusion of the herbal properties.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow gentian tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and redness in the eyes. Additionally, yellow gentian's natural antibacterial and antiviral compounds help to combat underlying infections or irritants that may be causing the itchiness. Anti-inflammatory effects: The anti-inflammatory compounds in Yellow Gentian tincture can help to reduce swelling and itching by reducing inflammation in the eyes.
Antibacterial properties: Yellow Gentian's antibacterial properties can help to combat bacterial infections that may contribute to itchy eyes, such as conjunctivitis or pink eye.

Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and terpenes, which provide anti-inflammatory properties to soothe itchy and irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest gentiana lutea roots by digging up 1 part of the root system per quart of tincture desired.
Clean and chop the harvested roots into small pieces for drying or processing.
Dry the chopped gentiana lutea roots in a warm place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine one part dried gentiana lutea roots with one part 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar and let it steep for 14 days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discard the solids, and store the finished tincture in a cool dark place.

Antioxidant: Marshmallow tincture's antioxidant properties can help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to chronic eye issues like itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm irritated eye tissue.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling in itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marshmallow roots weighing about 100 grams or dried ones in equal quantity for this recipe.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces and pack them loosely into clean glass jars.
Pour vodka with a 60% alcohol content filling the jar up to three-quarters full leaving enough space for liquid expansion.
Store the jar in a cool dark place allowing it to macerate for six weeks shaking it weekly to ensure thorough infusion.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Borage tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe irritated eyes and reduce redness. The herb's antioxidants also help to calm down any allergic reactions or sensitivities that may be causing the itchiness. Additionally, borage's natural antibacterial and antifungal properties can help to combat any underlying infections that could be contributing to itchy eyes. Overall, borage tinctures provide a safe and effective way to alleviate itchy eyes and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Borage tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and itching in the eyes by suppressing the release of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in borage tincture, such as gamma-linolenic acid (GLA), help neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to itchiness and irritation.

Demulcent properties: Borage tincture's demulcent properties create a protective film on the surface of the eyes, soothing and calming irritated tissues, and providing relief from itching and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Borage roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort.
Flowers: Borage flowers are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of itchy eyes such as redness and puffiness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh borago officinalis flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Chop the collected flowers into small pieces and combine with 50% vodka by weight.
Pack the herb mixture tightly into a clean glass jar leaving minimal headspace.
Store the jar at room temperature for 2-3 weeks to allow infusion and extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tinctures helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively soothe and calm irritated eyelids and conjunctiva. The tannins present in comfrey bind to mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, comfrey's antibacterial properties help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to itchiness. As a result, the tincture provides fast and gentle relief from itchy eyes, promoting a sense of comfort and clarity for those who suffer from this annoying condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness.
Antioxidant properties: Comfrey's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchy eye symptoms such as dryness and irritation.
Sothing and demulcent properties: Comfrey tincture's soothing and demulcent compounds, such as allantoin, help calm and moisturize the eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Comfrey roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing itching and discomfort.
Flowers: Comfrey flowers contain flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that work as natural antihistamines, providing relief from itchy eye symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of symphytum officinale and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and combine with 40% vodka or brandy in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container.
Store the filtered tincture in a dark glass bottle and label it with the date of preparation.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for itchy eyes are liquid extracts made from plants, flowers, and herbs that can provide natural relief from dry, irritated, or inflamed eyes. These tinctures help soothe itchy eyes by reducing inflammation, calming the mucous membranes, and moisturizing the eyes. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with itchy eyes include chamomile, calendula, and eyebright. For instance, using eyebright tincture can improve vision clarity and reduce itchiness caused by allergies or dry eye syndrome, allowing individuals to enjoy their daily activities without discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy eyes with herbal tinctures is by mixing a few drops of Eyebright tincture with distilled water and applying it directly to the affected area. This helps to reduce inflammation and itching. Alternatively, using Calendula or Stinging Nettle tinctures as an eye wash can also provide relief from itchiness and redness.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy eyes that are treated with herbal tinctures are skin irritations, such as eczema and psoriasis. Herbal tinctures containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and calendula have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm irritated skin and reduce itching and redness. Other conditions treated with herbal tinctures include insect bites, sunburn, and minor cuts and scrapes.
